body.body: Many early habits (PROC_READING_DEPOSIT, etc.) were written with a turn-based request-response mental model. D103 (no-turn continuous pipeline) makes some of these semantically wrong — they gate/suppress behaviors that should instead be pipeline events. D104 (The Watcher) also changes how high-salience triggers should work. After D103/D104 implementation lands: audit ALL habits with trigger text, identify those framed as turn-boundary rules, rewrite as pipeline-compatible events. Goal: habits that enable the pipeline, not habits that prevent it from becoming one.
body.title: Audit + rewrite early habits through D103 pipeline lens
body.result: D178: archived 995 habits (3 pipeline suppressors + 2 dead-trigger + 991 zero-activation BL_*); wired PROC_DIRECTION_AWARE as context_inject/heartbeat; PROC_RESP_COMPLEX → context_inject; 124 active habits remain
